description: Account in Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 1150–90 CE, based on Goitein's assessment. "The Collector of mezonot," money for bread for the poor. Payments of dirhems to communal officials made by that collector during three weeks. Of the eight persons listed under the second and third weeks, six recur also in the first, which contains fourteen names. The Nasi receives 7 dirhems twice, followed by R. Nissim with 4 dirhems in the second week, 3 in the third, and 2 in the first. Information from Goitein, Med Soc II, Appendix B, #73 (p. 460).
description_he: רשימה או טבלה ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הבריטית.
